title: polygala sibirica subsp. bhutanica
intro: Polygala sibirica subsp. bhutanica, also known as Bhutanese milkwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae) that is native to Bhutan. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and its extracts are often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, highlighting its potential as a valuable medicinal resource.
